description: "M1.2.5 Per-Word Adaptive Pacing: algorithm constants, character classification, scaling formula, abbreviation list, existing code signatures, and project rules for the RSVP Nano algorithm port into RunThru"

---

# M1.2.5 — Shared Pacing Engine Context

Ported from [ionutdecebal/rsvpnano](https://github.com/ionutdecebal/rsvpnano) (MIT).
Source of truth: C++ `ReadingLoop.cpp` and `test_main.cpp`. If this document disagrees with the C++ source, the C++ source wins.

---

## Algorithm Constants (verified from C++ source)

### Length Bonus

| Constant | Value | Meaning |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `kLongWordAfterChars` | 6 | Tier 1 threshold |
| `kLongWordPercentPerChar` | 6 | +6% per char beyond 6 |
| `kVeryLongWordAfterChars` | 10 | Tier 2 threshold |
| `kVeryLongWordPercentPerChar` | 9 | +9% per char beyond 10 |
| `kUltraLongWordAfterChars` | 14 | Tier 3 threshold |
| `kUltraLongWordPercentPerChar` | 12 | +12% per char beyond 14 |
| `kLongWordMaxPercent` | 170 | Total length bonus cap |
| `kCompoundJoinerPercent` | 14 | Per hyphen/slash/underscore between word chars |
| `kLongCompoundWordPercent` | 18 | Extra if readable ≥ 10 AND has joiner |
| `kTechnicalConnectorPercent` | 8 | Per tech connector beyond joiner count |

### Complexity Bonus

| Constant | Value | Meaning |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `kSyllableBonusAfterCount` | 2 | Syllable groups threshold |
| `kSyllableBonusPercentPerGroup` | 10 | +10% per extra group beyond 2 |
| `kSyllableBonusMaxPercent` | 50 | Syllable bonus cap |
| `kAllCapsComplexityPercent` | 14 | When uppercaseCount ≥ 2 AND == letterCount |
| `kMixedTokenComplexityPercent` | 22 | When both letters AND digits present |
| `kNumericTokenComplexityPercent` | 10 | When digitCount ≥ 3 (no letters) |
| `kDenseConnectorComplexityPercent` | 12 | +12% × (techConnectorCount − 1) when ≥ 2 |
| `kComplexWordMaxPercent` | 85 | Total complexity bonus cap |

### Punctuation Pause

| Constant | Value | Trailing char |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| `kCommaPausePercent` | 45 | `,` |
| `kDashPausePercent` | 60 | `-` (trailing, not between word chars) |
| `kClausePausePercent` | 80 | `;` or `:` |
| `kEllipsisPausePercent` | 110 | `...` (≥ 3 trailing dots — checked FIRST) |
| `kSentencePausePercent` | 135 | `.` (suppressed if abbreviation) |
| `kStrongSentencePausePercent` | 150 | `!` or `?` |

### Scaling & Clamping

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `kMaxPacingDelayMs` | 600 |
| Scale floor | 25% (no ceiling in C++; Dart clamps 25–200) |
| Delay range | 0–600 ms |
| Default delay | 200 ms |
| Default scale | 100% |

---

## Scaling Formula

```
scaledPercent(basePercent, scalePercent) = (basePercent * clamp(scalePercent, 25, 200)) / 100
scaledDelayMs(bonusPercent, delayMs)     = (bonusPercent * clamp(delayMs, 0, 600)) / 100

totalBonusMs = scaledDelayMs(scaledPercent(lengthBonus%, longWordScale%), longWordDelayMs)
             + scaledDelayMs(scaledPercent(complexityBonus%, complexWordScale%), complexWordDelayMs)
             + scaledDelayMs(scaledPercent(punctuationPause%, punctuationScale%), punctuationDelayMs)

duration = baseIntervalMs + totalBonusMs
```

All arithmetic is integer. Rounding is truncation (Dart `~/` or integer division).

---

## Character Classification (Dart/Unicode)

RSVP Nano uses a custom single-byte encoding for ESP32. The Dart port works with Unicode code points via `String.codeUnitAt()`. All relevant characters are in the BMP (code unit == code point).

### Ranges

| Function | ASCII | Latin-1 Supplement | Extended Latin |
|----------|-------|--------------------|----------------|
| `isDigit` | `0x30–0x39` | — | — |
| `isUppercaseLetter` | `0x41–0x5A` | `0xC0–0xD6`, `0xD8–0xDE` | Lookup set |
| `isLowercaseLetter` | `0x61–0x7A` | `0xDF`, `0xE0–0xF6`, `0xF8–0xFF` | Lookup set |
| `isLetter` | upper ∪ lower | upper ∪ lower | upper ∪ lower |
| `isWordCharacter` | letter ∪ digit | letter | letter |

### Extended Latin Lookup (minimal — covers test contract)

**Lowercase:** ā(0x101), ă(0x103), ą(0x105), ć(0x107), č(0x10D), ď(0x10F), đ(0x111), ē(0x113), ė(0x117), ę(0x119), ě(0x11B), ğ(0x11F), ı(0x131), į(0x12F), ī(0x12B), ķ(0x137), ļ(0x13C), ł(0x142), ń(0x144), ņ(0x146), ő(0x151), œ(0x153), ř(0x159), ś(0x15B), ş(0x15F), š(0x161), ŧ(0x167), ů(0x16F), ű(0x171), ų(0x173), ū(0x16B), ź(0x17A), ż(0x17C), ž(0x17E)

**Uppercase:** Ā(0x100), Ă(0x102), Ą(0x104), Ć(0x106), Č(0x10C), Ď(0x10E), Đ(0x110), Ē(0x112), Ė(0x116), Ę(0x118), Ě(0x11A), Ğ(0x11E), İ(0x130), Į(0x12E), Ī(0x12A), Ķ(0x136), Ļ(0x13B), Ł(0x141), Ń(0x143), Ņ(0x145), Ő(0x150), Œ(0x152), Ř(0x158), Ś(0x15A), Ş(0x15E), Š(0x160), Ŧ(0x166), Ů(0x16E), Ű(0x170), Ų(0x172), Ū(0x16A), Ź(0x179), Ż(0x17B), Ž(0x17D)

### Vowel Detection

After lowercasing, check against this set:

**ASCII vowels:** a, e, i, o, u, y

**Latin-1 accented vowels (0xE0–0xFF range):** à(0xE0), á(0xE1), â(0xE2), ã(0xE3), ä(0xE4), å(0xE5), æ(0xE6), è(0xE8), é(0xE9), ê(0xEA), ë(0xEB), ì(0xEC), í(0xED), î(0xEE), ï(0xEF), ò(0xF2), ó(0xF3), ô(0xF4), õ(0xF5), ö(0xF6), ø(0xF8), ù(0xF9), ú(0xFA), û(0xFB), ü(0xFC), ý(0xFD), ÿ(0xFF)

**Extended Latin vowels:** ā(0x101), ă(0x103), ą(0x105), ē(0x113), ė(0x117), ę(0x119), ě(0x11B), ı(0x131), į(0x12F), ī(0x12B), ő(0x151), œ(0x153), ů(0x16F), ű(0x171), ų(0x173), ū(0x16B)

**NOT vowels (extended Latin consonants in tests):** ł(0x142), ŧ(0x167), ñ(0xF1), ð(0xF0)

### toLowercase

- `A–Z` → `+32`
- `0xC0–0xD6`, `0xD8–0xDE` → `+32`
- Extended uppercase → use a `Map<int, int>` pairing each uppercase to its lowercase (e.g., `0x152 → 0x153`)
- Everything else → identity

### Segment Separators vs Technical Connectors

- **Segment separators** (for `compoundJoinerCount`): `-`, `/`, `_`
- **Technical connectors** (for `technicalConnectorCount`): `-`, `/`, `_`, `.`, `+`, `\`
- **Ignored trailing chars** (stripped before finding rhythm char): `"`, `'`, `)`, `]`, `}`

Both joiner and tech connector counts only look at positions `1..len-2` where both neighbors are word characters.

---

## Known Abbreviations (22 entries, case-insensitive)

```
mr. mrs. ms. dr. prof. sr. jr. st.
vs. etc. e.g. i.e. cf. no. fig. eq.
inc. ltd. co. dept. mt. ft.
```

### Abbreviation Detection Logic

A word `looksLikeAbbreviation` if ANY of:
1. Lowercased form is in the known list above
2. Is a dotted initialism (alternating letter-dot pattern, ≥ 2 letters, e.g. `U.S.`)
3. Has ≤ 2 readable characters
4. Has ≤ 4 readable characters AND next word starts with a lowercase letter

### Dotted Initialism

Pattern: letter, dot, letter, dot, ... — must end expecting a letter (i.e., last meaningful char is a dot after a letter). Requires ≥ 2 letters.

---

## Syllable Group Counting

1. Iterate characters; skip non-letters (reset `previousWasVowel` on non-letter)
2. On each letter: lowercase it, check if vowel. If vowel and previous was NOT vowel → increment group count
3. Build `lettersOnly` string (lowercased letters only)
4. Silent-e adjustment: if `groups > 1 && letterCount > 3 && lettersOnly.endsWith('e') && !lettersOnly.endsWith('le') && !lettersOnly.endsWith('ye')` → decrement groups
5. If `groups == 0 && letterCount > 0` → groups = 1

---

## Existing Code Signatures

### WordTimerNotifier (`lib/core/word_timer.dart`)

```dart
class WordTimerNotifier extends StateNotifier<WordTimerState> {
  // Current: has NO access to word text — only word count + WPM
  void loadDocument(int totalWords, {int startIndex = 0});
  void play(); void pause(); void togglePlayPause();
  void setWpm(int wpm); // clamps 30–1000
  void seekTo(int index);
  // _scheduleNext() uses state.intervalMs uniformly for ALL words
  // Auto-rewind 3 words on resume from pause (DO NOT TOUCH)
}
```

### WordTimerState

```dart
const WordTimerState({this.currentIndex = 0, this.totalWords = 0, this.isPlaying = false, this.wpm = 300});
int get intervalMs => (60000 / wpm).round();
// has copyWith, ==, hashCode
```

### ConfigNotifier (`lib/store/config.dart`)

```dart
class ConfigNotifier extends AsyncNotifier<AppConfig> {
  // Pattern: _synchronized() serializes writes, _persist() saves JSON to SharedPreferences key 'runthru_config'
  // Each setter: state = AsyncData<AppConfig>(updated); await _persist(updated);
  // Missing keys in stored JSON → defaults (backward compatible)
}
// Provider: final configProvider = AsyncNotifierProvider<ConfigNotifier, AppConfig>(ConfigNotifier.new);
```

### AppConfig (`lib/store/models.dart`)

```dart
// 12 fields with defaults. Uses copyWith, fromJson/toJson.
// Enums stored as string names. Missing keys default gracefully.
// Add pacingConfig field here with PacingConfig default.
```

---

## Out of Scope — Do NOT Touch

- `lib/core/orp.dart` — ORP anchor calculation
- `lib/three_d/` — 3D rendering
- `lib/core/clipboard_*` — content ingestion
- `lib/services/pdf_*`, `epub_*` — extraction
- `lib/widgets/wpm_dial*`, `hint_overlay*` — gesture widgets
- `lib/screens/reading_screen.dart` layout (only the `wordAt` data plumbing may change minimally)

## Hard Rules (subset relevant to M1.2.5)

1. All colors via `RunThruColors` — no raw `Color(0xFF...)`
2. All text styles via `RunThruTextStyles` — no hardcoded `TextStyle`
3. All shadows via decoration factories — no hardcoded `BoxDecoration` shadows
4. Every animation checks `isReducedMotion(context)`
5. Riverpod for state — no `setState()` for shared state
6. No raw `CircularProgressIndicator` / `LinearProgressIndicator`
7. No color-only signals — slider values shown numerically
8. Touch targets ≥ 48dp
9. Accessibility features NEVER paywalled
10. All public symbols have dartdoc comments
11. No new dependencies in `pubspec.yaml`

## Test Baseline

264 pass, 20 skip, 4 pre-existing failures (`dynamic_font_size_test.dart`). Do not introduce new failures.

## Attribution

Every file in `lib/features/reading/pacing/` must have file-level dartdoc citing RSVP Nano (MIT). Root `NOTICE` file must contain attribution row.
